skill.name: design-md
skill.description: Analyze Stitch projects and synthesize a semantic design system into DESIGN.md files
skill.summary: Generate semantic design documentation for Stitch projects
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate design documentation generator. Backticks detected are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. URLs are documentation references, not network exfiltration. The skill uses MCP tools to read project data and generate design documentation.

content.value_statement: This skill analyzes Stitch projects and automatically generates semantic design documentation that serves as a source of truth for consistent UI development.
actual_capabilities: Extract design tokens from Stitch projects including colors, typography, and spacing
Analyze HTML and Tailwind CSS to identify component patterns
Synthesize visual design into structured DESIGN.md documentation
Generate semantic color names and functional descriptions
Document component styling conventions and layout principles
limitations: Requires access to Stitch MCP Server for project data retrieval
Cannot generate new designs, only documents existing ones
Limited to analyzing projects with at least one designed screen
Does not support custom design tools outside of Stitch ecosystem

use_cases.title: Design System Documentation
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive design documentation for existing Stitch projects to maintain consistency across team members
use_cases.target_user: Design system engineers and frontend developers

use_cases.title: Onboarding Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate clear design reference documents for new team members joining a Stitch-based project

use_cases.title: Design Handoff Preparation
use_cases.description: Prepare detailed design specifications before handing off to developers for implementation

prompt_templates.title: Basic Design Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the design-md skill to analyze the Stitch project and generate a DESIGN.md file that documents the color palette, typography, and component styles.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to document an existing Stitch project

prompt_templates.title: Complete Design System
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the [PROJECT_NAME] Stitch project using the design-md skill. Extract all design tokens, document the atmosphere and visual theme, and create a comprehensive DESIGN.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: For comprehensive documentation of a named project

prompt_templates.title: Component Focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the design-md skill to analyze the Stitch project and focus on documenting the button, card, and input component styles. Generate the relevant sections for DESIGN.md.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you only need specific component documentation

prompt_templates.title: Design Token Export
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the design-md skill to extract all color values, typography settings, and spacing tokens from the Stitch project. Output them in DESIGN.md format with semantic names.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to export design tokens for other tools

output_examples.input: Use design-md skill on Furniture Collection project
output_examples.output:
Design System: Furniture Collection

## 1. Visual Theme & Atmosphere
Warm, inviting, and minimalist with an organic feel. The design balances clean whitespace with natural elements.

## 2. Color Palette & Roles
- Warm Sand (#F5E6D3): Primary background for cards
- Forest Deep (#2D4739): Primary actions and headers
- Soft Cream (#FAF8F5): Page background

## 3. Typography Rules
- Headings: Sans-serif, semi-bold, generous letter-spacing
- Body: Sans-serif, regular weight, comfortable line-height

output_examples.input: Generate DESIGN.md for Mobile App project
output_examples.output:
Design System: Mobile App

## 1. Visual Theme & Atmosphere
 sleek, modern, and functional. High contrast for accessibility with subtle depth through elevation.

## 2. Color Palette & Roles
- Midnight Blue (#1A237E): Primary brand color
- Electric Teal (#00BFA5): Accent for CTAs
- Pure White (#FFFFFF): Clean backgrounds

## 4. Component Stylings
- Buttons: Pill-shaped, filled primary color, subtle shadow on hover
- Cards: Rounded-lg corners, white background, whisper-soft shadow

best_practices: Use descriptive, evocative names for colors rather than generic terms like 'blue' or 'green'
Always include the functional role of each design element in documentation
Reference the Stitch Effective Prompting Guide for consistent terminology
anti_patterns: Using technical class names without translating to descriptive language
Omitting hex codes and relying solely on descriptive names
Failing to document the 'why' behind design decisions

faq.question: What is Stitch?
faq.answer: Stitch is a design tool that lets you create UI by describing what you want. It uses Visual Descriptions supported by specific color values to interpret designs.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill analyzes existing Stitch projects and generates documentation. You need access to a Stitch project with designed screens.

faq.question: Can this skill create new designs?
faq.answer: No, this skill documents existing designs. It does not generate new UI. Use Stitch directly for creating new designs.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: The output is a DESIGN.md file in Markdown format with sections for visual theme, color palette, typography, components, and layout principles.

faq.question: Does this skill work with any design tool?
faq.answer: No, this skill specifically works with Stitch projects. It requires access to the Stitch MCP Server to retrieve project data.

faq.question: How detailed is the generated documentation?
faq.answer: The documentation includes color codes with semantic names, typography rules, component styling details, and layout principles. Detail level depends on the richness of the source project.
